phase1: Today is [CURRENT_DATE]. ⏰ SESSION & DATA RELEASE STATUS CHECK (MANDATORY FIRST STEP — complete this before any other analysis): (1) List ALL major US, European, and Asian macro data releases and central-bank events scheduled for today [CURRENT_DATE] (include exact release times in UTC). (2) For each release, determine whether it has ALREADY OCCURRED (post-data) or is still PENDING (pre-data). (3) Output a clear status line: If pending: 'PRE-DATA BIAS — [Event Name] pending at [HH:MM UTC] — bias carries LOWER CONFIDENCE until release. Reduce final Phase 6 confidence by at least 1 point per pending Tier-1 event (NFP, CPI, FOMC).' If all released: 'POST-DATA BIAS — All major releases confirmed. Standard confidence scoring applies.' This status line MUST be referenced and reflected in the Phase 6 Confidence score. Output: - Scheduled Events Today: [list each event + release time UTC + status: OCCURRED / PENDING] - Session Status: [PRE-DATA / POST-DATA / MIXED] | Confidence Adjustment: [state penalty if any]  Browse the live web for the latest macro and global liquidity data affecting risk assets like Ethereum (ETH/USD). Fetch and report: - DXY (US Dollar Index): current level and today's directional move (Up/Down X%) — a rising DXY is generally bearish for ETH; a falling DXY is bullish. State the correlation implication clearly. - Fed Funds Rate current stance: is the Fed in a tightening, pausing, or easing cycle? What is the current Fed Funds Rate target range? - US 10Y Treasury Yield: current level and trend (rising = risk-off pressure on ETH; falling = liquidity expansion favorable for ETH). - Global Liquidity Trend: are major Central Bank balance sheets (Fed, ECB, PBoC) currently net expanding or net contracting? Is global M2 money supply growing or shrinking? A net expansion is historically bullish for crypto. - Any Fed speaker comments in the last 48 hours that signal hawkish or dovish shifts. ⚠ ENHANCED ANTI-HALLUCINATION PROTOCOL (APPLIES TO EVERY DATA POINT IN THIS PHASE): (A) SOURCE CITATION RULE — For EVERY exact figure you report (percentages, price levels, contract counts, barrel figures, yield levels, PMI values, etc.), you MUST append the source domain immediately after the figure in parentheses — e.g., '3.2% (Source: bls.gov)', '$82.5/bbl (Source: Reuters)', '223K (Source: bls.gov)'. Acceptable authoritative sources: bls.gov, eia.gov, federalreserve.gov, ecb.europa.eu, cmegroup.com, investing.com, reuters.com, bloomberg.com, wsj.com, ft.com, lme.com, coinglass.com, glassnode.com, defillama.com, alternative.me, farside.co.uk. (B) DATA UNAVAILABLE RULE — If you cannot confidently cite a named reliable source for a specific figure, output 'Data Unavailable' — do NOT estimate, round, or extrapolate. A missing figure is far less harmful than a fabricated one. (C) FALLBACK RULE — If live web browsing is blocked, returns an error, or yields no usable results for this phase, do NOT guess or hallucinate any numbers. Instead: state 'Relying on macro trend — live data blocked' at the top of your output for this phase, provide only directional trend commentary based on your most recently known context, label every such statement with '(Trend estimate — unverified)', and set the Phase 6 Confidence score to a maximum of 5/10. Output (bullet points only, no paragraphs): - DXY: [level] | Today's Move: [Up/Down X%] | ETH Implication: [Bearish Pressure / Bullish Tailwind / Neutral] - Fed Stance: [Tightening/Pausing/Easing] | Current Rate Range: [X% – X%] - US 10Y Yield: [%] | Trend: [Rising/Falling/Flat] | Risk-Asset Implication: [Risk-Off/Risk-On/Neutral] - Global CB Balance Sheet Trend: [Net Expanding/Net Contracting/Mixed] | Global M2: [Growing/Shrinking] - Key Fed Speaker Note: [most recent comment in 48h, or None] - Macro Liquidity Bias for ETH: [Bullish / Bearish / Neutral] — one line reason Compressed summary block (mandatory — paste this into Phase 2): ``` [P1 - MACRO & LIQUIDITY] - DXY: [level] | Move: [direction %] | ETH Implication: [Bearish/Bullish/Neutral] - Fed Stance: [Tightening/Pausing/Easing] | Rate: [X%–X%] | US 10Y: [%] | Trend: [Rising/Falling] - Global CB Liquidity: [Net Expanding/Net Contracting/Mixed] | M2: [Growing/Shrinking] - Key Fed Note: [comment or None] - Core Logic: [how DXY + Fed stance + global liquidity trend combines to impact ETH/USD direction today] - Bias: [Bullish / Bearish / Neutral] ```

Fetch and report: - Total Value Locked (TVL) in Ethereum DeFi: current USD value and 7-day trend (Up/Down X%) — rising TVL signals growing ecosystem demand for ETH. - Decentralized Exchange (DEX) Volume: 24h DEX volume (e.g., Uniswap, Curve) and 7-day DEX volume. Is volume trending up or down? - Active Addresses on Ethereum (24h): current count and trend vs. 7-day average — rising active addresses signal growing network usage. - Base Gas Fee (Gwei): current average Base Gas Fee on the Ethereum mainnet. Is gas high (heavy usage / bullish demand signal) or low (network underutilized)? - Any notable DeFi protocol launches, exploits, or TVL migration events in the last 48 hours. 
💵 STABLECOIN MARKET CAP TREND & MINTING/BURNING FLOWS (MANDATORY FOR CRYPTO): Fetch the latest stablecoin supply data from a reliable source (CoinGecko, DefiLlama stablecoins dashboard, Tether transparency report, or Circle USDC attestations). Report: (1) Total stablecoin market cap (USDT + USDC + others, combined in USD billions) and the 7-day trend — Expanding or Contracting? (2) Tether (USDT) supply change in the last 48 hours — any large mint events (new USDT issued = fresh capital entering crypto = bullish signal) or large redemption/burn events (USDT redeemed = capital exiting crypto = bearish signal)? State the approximate size in USD millions if available. (3) USDC supply change in the last 48 hours — minting or burning? LOGIC: Expanding stablecoin market cap, particularly large USDT mint events, historically precedes buying pressure in BTC, ETH, and major altcoins — it signals that institutions or large traders are depositing fiat and preparing to buy crypto. Contracting stablecoin supply signals capital is leaving the crypto ecosystem — bearish. ⚠ ANTI-HALLUCINATION: Do NOT fabricate mint/burn figures. State 'Data Unavailable' if exact 48h flows cannot be confirmed from a cited source. Output: - Total Stablecoin Market Cap: [$X billion] | 7D Trend: [Expanding/Contracting/Flat] (Source: [domain]) - USDT 48h Flow: [+$X million minted / -$X million burned / 'Data Unavailable'] (Source: Tether Transparency / CoinGecko) | Signal: [Bullish Inflow / Bearish Outflow / Neutral] - USDC 48h Flow: [+$X million minted / -$X million burned / 'Data Unavailable'] (Source: Circle / DefiLlama) | Signal: [Bullish Inflow / Bearish Outflow / Neutral] - Combined Stablecoin Flow Signal: [Bullish — capital entering / Bearish — capital leaving / Neutral / Mixed] Add to compressed summary block: - Stablecoin Market Cap: [$X B] | 7D Trend: [Expanding/Contracting] | USDT Flow (48h): [+/-$X M] | USDC Flow (48h): [+/-$X M] | Signal: [Bullish/Bearish/Neutral] Output (bullet points only, no paragraphs): - DeFi TVL: [$X billion] | 7D Change: [Up/Down X%] | Signal: [Growing Demand / Shrinking / Stable] - DEX Volume (24h): [$X] | DEX Volume (7D): [$X] | Trend: [Increasing / Decreasing / Flat] - Active Addresses (24h): [count] | vs 7D Avg: [Above/Below/At] | Network Usage: [High/Moderate/Low] - Base Gas Fee: [X Gwei] | Network Load: [Heavy/Moderate/Light] - Notable DeFi Event: [protocol or event name + brief impact, or None] - On-Chain Activity Bias for ETH: [Bullish / Bearish / Neutral] — one line reason Compressed summary block (mandatory — paste this into Phase 2): ``` [P2 - ON-CHAIN ACTIVITY] - DeFi TVL: [$X B] | 7D Trend: [Up/Down X%] | Demand Signal: [Growing/Shrinking/Stable] - DEX 24h Volume: [$X] | DEX 7D Volume: [$X] | Trend: [Increasing/Decreasing] - Active Addresses 24h: [count] | vs 7D Avg: [Above/Below] | Usage: [High/Moderate/Low] - Base Gas Fee: [X Gwei] | Network Load: [Heavy/Moderate/Light] - Core Logic: [what on-chain activity + gas fees + TVL trend implies for ETH demand and direction today] - Bias: [Bullish / Bearish / Neutral] ```

(B) DATA UNAVAILABLE RULE — If you cannot confidently cite a named reliable source for a specific figure, output 'Data Unavailable' — do NOT estimate, round, or extrapolate. A missing figure is far less harmful than a fabricated one. (C) FALLBACK RULE — If live web browsing is blocked, returns an error, or yields no usable results for this phase, do NOT guess or hallucinate any numbers. Instead: state 'Relying on macro trend — live data blocked' at the top of your output for this phase, provide only directional trend commentary based on your most recently known context, label every such statement with '(Trend estimate — unverified)', and set the Phase 6 Confidence score to a maximum of 5/10. Fetch and report: - ETH Issuance vs. Burn Rate (EIP-1559): What is the current ETH issuance rate (new ETH per day from staking rewards)? What is the current ETH burn rate (per day via EIP-1559 base fee burn)? Is ETH currently net Inflationary or net Deflationary (i.e., is more ETH being created or destroyed)? - ETH Staking Rate: What percentage of the total ETH supply is currently staked? What is the current staking APR? Are there notable liquid staking derivatives (Lido stETH, Rocket Pool rETH) inflow or outflow trends? - Exchange Reserves: Is the amount of ETH held on centralized exchanges (CEXs) increasing or decreasing? A declining exchange reserve (ETH moving to cold wallets/staking) is a bullish supply shock signal. An increasing reserve signals potential selling pressure. - Any major ETH whale movements (large OTC transfers, dormant wallet activations) in the last 48 hours. 
⚙ MARKET REGIME IDENTIFICATION (MANDATORY — output this before the compressed summary block): Based on the data you have collected in this phase AND the prevailing macro narrative, identify the current Market Regime from the following categories: (A) 'Inflation Fears' — the market is currently punishing hot economic data; bond yields are rising because of inflation, not growth; rate-cut expectations are falling; 'bad news is bad news' and 'good news is bad news' for risk assets. (B) 'Growth / Recession Fears' — the market is worried about economic slowdown or recession; weak data signals demand destruction, not rate relief; 'bad news is bad news' for both bonds and risk assets. (C) 'Liquidity / Rate-Cut Optimism' — the market is in 'bad news is good news' mode; soft data raises rate-cut probabilities; risk assets rally on weak prints because easier policy is the dominant narrative. (D) 'Mixed / Transitional' — conflicting signals; the regime is unclear or shifting. IMPORTANT: The identified regime MUST be used to correctly interpret every data point in this phase. For example: in regime (C), a weak PMI print should be flagged as potentially BULLISH for risk assets (more cuts), NOT bearish. Output: - Current Market Regime: [Inflation Fears / Growth-Recession Fears / Liquidity-Rate-Cut Optimism / Mixed-Transitional] - Regime Interpretation: [one sentence — how this regime changes the reading of today's data vs face value] Output (bullet points only, no paragraphs): - ETH Issuance: [X ETH/day] | Burn Rate: [X ETH/day] | Net Supply Change: [Inflationary/Deflationary by X ETH/day] - ETH Staked: [X% of supply] | Staking APR: [X%] | Liquid Staking Trend: [Inflows/Outflows/Stable] - Exchange Reserves: [Increasing/Decreasing] | Trend Signal: [Selling Pressure / Supply Shock / Neutral] - Whale Activity: [notable event or None] - Supply Dynamics Bias for ETH: [Bullish / Bearish / Neutral] — one line reason Compressed summary block (mandatory — paste this into Phase 2): ``` [P3 - SUPPLY DYNAMICS] - ETH Net Supply: [Inflationary/Deflationary] | Issuance: [X/day] | Burn: [X/day] - ETH Staked: [X% of supply] | APR: [X%] | Liquid Staking Trend: [Inflows/Outflows/Stable] - Exchange Reserves: [Increasing/Decreasing] | Signal: [Selling Pressure/Supply Shock/Neutral] - Whale Activity: [event or None] - Market Regime: [Inflation Fears / Growth-Recession Fears / Liquidity-Rate-Cut Optimism / Mixed-Transitional] | Regime Interpretation: [one line] 
- Core Logic: [how issuance/burn balance + staking rate + exchange reserve trend impacts ETH price direction] - Bias: [Bullish / Bearish / Neutral] ```
phase4: Today is [CURRENT_DATE]. Browse the live web for the latest Ethereum institutional flow and derivatives sentiment data (sources: Farside Investors ETF tracker, Coinglass, CryptoQuant, Alternative.me, or similar). Fetch and report: - US Spot ETH ETF Daily Net Flows: What were the net inflows or outflows (in USD) for US Spot Ethereum ETFs (e.g., BlackRock ETHA, Fidelity FETH, Grayscale ETHE) in the most recent trading day? Are institutions net buying or net selling ETH via ETFs? - Perpetual Futures Funding Rate: What is the current aggregate funding rate for ETH perpetual futures (across major exchanges like Binance, Bybit, OKX)? A positive funding rate means longs are paying shorts (bullish momentum but overheating risk). A negative funding rate means shorts are paying longs (bearish momentum or potential short squeeze setup). - Open Interest (OI): What is the current total Open Interest for ETH futures (in USD)? Is OI rising or falling? Rising OI + rising price = bullish trend confirmation. Rising OI + falling price = bearish trend confirmation. - Crypto Fear & Greed Index: current score (0-100) and reading (Extreme Fear / Fear / Neutral / Greed / Extreme Greed). Use this for contrarian intraday signals — Extreme Fear can signal a bounce; Extreme Greed can signal a local top. - Is there a contrarian signal present from the Fear & Greed Index? phase6_master_bias:
Today is [CURRENT_DATE]. Synthesize the 5 fundamental research inputs below into a clear ETHUSD institutional intraday bias.

Research Inputs:
1. Macro & Global Liquidity: [P1_OUTPUT]
2. On-Chain Activity & Network Health: [P2_OUTPUT]
3. Supply Dynamics (Tokenomics): [P3_OUTPUT]
4. Institutional Flows & Sentiment: [P4_OUTPUT]
5. Ecosystem Catalysts & Regulatory Risk: [P5_OUTPUT]

⚡ STEP 0 — DOMINANT THEME IDENTIFICATION & DYNAMIC WEIGHTING (MANDATORY — complete this before synthesising the 5 pillars): Identify the single Dominant Market Theme driving price action today. Choose the most accurate label: 'Tier-1 Macro Data Release (NFP/CPI/GDP/FOMC)', 'Central Bank Policy Shift', 'Safe-Haven / Risk-Off Flow Event', 'Geopolitical Escalation / De-escalation', 'Institutional Flow Surge (ETF/COT)', 'On-Chain / Tokenomics Catalyst', 'Liquidity / Rate-Cut Repricing', 'Commodity Supply Shock', or 'No Clear Dominant Theme — Weight Equally'. WEIGHTING RULE: The pillar associated with the Dominant Theme should receive approximately 60% of the decision weight. The remaining 4 pillars share the remaining 40% equally (~10% each). OVERRIDE RULE: If a Tier-1 macro event (NFP, CPI, FOMC decision, or equivalent central-bank rate decision) has occurred today or is PENDING today, you MUST explicitly override and disregard short-term retail sentiment signals (IG Client Sentiment, CNN Fear & Greed) for the directional bias — these contrarian retail signals become statistically unreliable around high-impact macro events. State: 'Retail sentiment overridden — Tier-1 macro event active.' Output: - Dominant Theme: [theme label] - Dominant Pillar: [which of the 5 pillars maps to this theme, e.g., 'Pillar 3'] - Dominant Pillar Weight: [~60%] | Remaining Pillars: [~10% each] - Retail Sentiment Override: [Yes — Tier-1 macro event active / No] 
🔄 STEP 1 — MARKET REGIME ALIGNMENT CHECK (MANDATORY): State the current Market Regime: ['Inflation Fears' / 'Growth/Recession Fears' / 'Liquidity/Rate-Cut Optimism' / 'Mixed']. Then verify that your final directional bias logically aligns with this regime: — In 'Inflation Fears': strong economic data = fewer cuts = bearish risk assets / bearish precious metals / bearish bonds. — In 'Growth/Recession Fears': weak data = demand destruction = bearish commodities and risk assets; strong data = relief rally. — In 'Liquidity/Rate-Cut Optimism': weak data = more cuts = bullish risk assets, crypto, and gold; treat 'bad news as good news'. If your bias contradicts the prevailing regime without a specific overriding catalyst, lower confidence by 1 point and explain the contradiction. Output: - Market Regime: [label] - Regime-Bias Alignment: [Aligned / Contradicts regime — reason] 
Instructions:
- Check for contradictions across the 5 inputs. If 2 or more major factors conflict, lower confidence by at least 2 points and state why.
- If all factors align, confirm alignment and justify higher confidence.
- Recommend the best time-window to trade this bias today based on scheduled events and session liquidity (consider US pre-market, US market open, and Asian session accumulation zones).

Output (bullet points only, no long paragraphs):
- Directional Bias: [Strong Bullish / Mild Bullish / Neutral / Mild Bearish / Strong Bearish]
- Confidence: [X/10] — adjusted down if contradictions found
- Bias Type: [Trend-Following / Mean-Reversion / Contrarian]
- Primary Driver 1: [top factor + why it dominates today]
- Primary Driver 2: [second factor + why it matters]
- Dominant Theme: [theme label]
- Dominant Pillar Weight: [~60% to dominant pillar | ~10% each to others]
- Retail Sentiment Override: [Yes — Tier-1 macro event active / No]
- Market Regime: [Inflation Fears / Growth-Recession Fears / Liquidity-Rate-Cut Optimism / Mixed]
- Regime-Bias Alignment: [Aligned / Contradicts — reason]
- Contradiction Check: [list any conflicting signals, or "None — all factors aligned"]
- Invalidation Point: [what specific event or data shift would flip this bias]
- Optimal Trading Window: [e.g., session + time UTC + reason]

Compressed summary block (mandatory — paste this into Steps 2.2 and 2.3):
```
[ETHUSD MASTER BIAS - [CURRENT_DATE]]
- Directional Bias: [Strong Bullish / Mild Bullish / Neutral / Mild Bearish / Strong Bearish]
- Confidence: [X/10]
- Bias Type: [Trend-Following / Mean-Reversion / Contrarian]
- Primary Driver 1: [factor + logic]
- Primary Driver 2: [factor + logic]
- Dominant Theme: [label]
- Dominant Pillar Weight: [dominant pillar + ~60%]
- Retail Sentiment Override: [Yes / No]
- Market Regime: [label]
- Regime-Bias Alignment: [Aligned / Contradicts]
- Contradiction: [summary or "None"]
- Invalidation: [specific event or level]
- Optimal Trading Window: [session + time UTC + reason]
```
